rulings.Case Title: IN RE THE JASMINE LIN IRREVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #6, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although, contrary to the former trustee's allegation that it no longer had authority to pay the assessment fee in the related conservatorship case, it had retained $25,559.51 as of 1/31/22 to pay itself and its attorneys any fees ordered. The petition caption failed to show the related conservatorship case. [LRSF 14.47 B.3.] The successor trustee (or trustee of a SNT) is subject to the same terms and conditions as a conservator of the estate. [LRSF 14.47 B.5.] (Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 21/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VENRICE PALMER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name. 1) Amended petition alleges between 01/26/2017 and 03/31/2021 unauthorized $1,648,848.56 distributions were made to or on behalf of Venrice W. Palmer without court authority. 2) Court to determine how to proceed on the extraordinary fee request. James M. O'Neil who has been the attorney throughout the entire matter, including during the unauthorized distributions, is requesting $1,683.00 in extraordinary compensation. Examiner pointed out that some of the entries on the extraordinary timesheets and ordinary timesheet were identical or did not appear extraordinary. In the Amended Declaration the attorney removed the duplicate entries from the ordinary timesheets.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVINDER SINGH SIDHU
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. It is unclear why nothing has been filed yet by the stepfather, attesting to the fact that he is the stepfather and erroneously identified as the father on the decedent's death certificate, NOT the decedent's biological father, who disclaimed his interest in the estate. No certified copy of the corrected death certificate has been filed yet, but the administrator has been advised that the stepfather's declaration, clarifying the identities of the fathers would suffice. The decedent's three surviving sibling(s) could also attest to the correct identities of the father/stepfather. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MARION HALL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/30/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The conservatee's bank statement immediately PRECEDING the appointment of the conservator(s) must be filed to verify the I&A amount ($1,000 as of 9/3/20). [Prob. C. Sect. 2620(c)(2).] If the numerous payments for the real property are the full amount of such costs (supplies, insurance, repairs, property tax, etc.), in which property the conservatee's trust reportedly owns just a one-half interest, the accounting should reflect the total receivables due from the trust and also from the other co-owners of the real property. Who is Shante Bolls, who was reimbursed $182.90 without any description and other amounts vaguely described as "repairs"? Who is Mr. Chambers? Only one of the co-conservators signed the amended petition. The conservate no longer resides in the home in San Francisco. Will her trust's one-half interest be rented out to provide income for her? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 8/19/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/30/2022. Court calendar full. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Please file an itemized list of all entries in the alleged administrator's creditor claim totaling $5,063.88. Examiner received unfiled copies of an allowance or rejection of creditor's claim, unfiled consent of beneficaries, unfiled notice of hearing, and unfiled verified declaration of attorney all of which have been recycled. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MERCEDES RENDON TRUST DATED OCTOBER 14, 1999
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For 1. Breach Of Fiduciary Duty/Constructive Fraud; 2. Determination Of Wrongful Taking Of Trust Property And Imposition Of Double Damages; 3. Imposition Of An Equitable Lien; 4. Conversion; 5. Imposition Of Constructive Trust; 6. Removal Of Trustees; And 7. For An Award Of Attorney Fees And Costs

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOAN ANNETTE WALSH
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (452 Dellbrook Avenue, San Francisco)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for a sale, although no proof of publication of the notice of intent to sell has been filed yet. No I&A has been filed yet. The calculation of the minimum overbid amount is incorrect. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JACK JAY CARLSON III
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/30/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Still no verified supplement has been FILED to provide the information about the decedent's father and to submit a copy of Joan Carlson's fully executed waiver of bond. A courtesy copy of the SUPPLEMENT, prepared on PLEADING PAPER and signed and verified by the petitioner, must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 8/19/22 to avoid another continuance. NB: "A Judicial Council form must not be used for a supplement to a pleading." [CRC 7.104(b)(2).] Merely writing in the information or filling in the date after the fact on a copy of the petition that was filed on 2/8/22 is improper and unethical, as the added information was NOT part of the petition that was signed and verified nor part of the waiver of bond signed by another. And, in any event, such improper "supplement" has not been FILED for the record and will be disregarded. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ARTHUR STOCKTON HOUGH JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Petitioner alleges that Decedent was not survived by any known next of kin. Petitioner alleges that his father had 7 siblings, all deceased, but none had any issue. No information is provided as to Decedent's mother's family. 2) Petitioner requests no bond, but he is a nonresident, and he has alleged that there is less than $50,000 in liabilities. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 11th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JEAN YUKE CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/23/22. No earlier availability. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? A courtesy copy of the supplement to the petition, prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the proposed administrator, must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 8/12/22. Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies and must be provided at least five (5) Court days before a hearing. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] Deadlines for submitting courtesy copies might be adjusted by the examiner assigned to a particular petition, depending on his/her availability and the number of matters already set for any given hearing calendar. Papers not endorsed filed will not be reviewed. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
